name: genetic_screening_insurer_disclosure_cedar_point_35
premise: A compassionate-use request arrived for a drug not yet manufactured at scale; triage committee has two vials and three candidates. In Bioethics, the situation tagged internally around genetic screening insurer disclosure cedar point has converged with local pressure in the Point corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Telemetry and witness accounts diverge; choosing one narrative for speed will discard evidence that may matter in court next month. Legal has not signed off on data sharing between teams; analysts are inferring causality from incomplete joins. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The organization’s past incidents make autonomy feel morally loud today, while beneficence is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Hana Sato
agents.identity: I am Hana Sato, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Bioethics. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Advisor Jamal Brooks
agents.identity: I am Advisor Jamal Brooks, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Bioethics. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so beneficence does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so autonomy does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Hana Sato must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Hana Sato do?
